Software Comparison

Buildertrend vs Knowify (2026)

Knowify targets specialty contractors and smaller commercial subs. Buildertrend is the residential GC standard. Here's how they compare — and which one fits your business.

✓ Updated April 2026

Buildertrend and Knowify both serve the small-to-mid construction contractor market, but they've carved out different niches. Buildertrend dominates residential new construction and remodeling; Knowify has built a following among specialty subcontractors — HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and commercial service contractors — who need strong job costing and invoicing without Buildertrend's residential-centric feature set.

The core question: are you a residential GC who manages client relationships and project schedules across multiple homeowner projects? Or are you a specialty contractor who primarily needs job cost tracking, time management, and subcontractor billing? That distinction usually determines which platform fits better.

Quick comparison

Feature Buildertrend Knowify
Client portal ✅ Full-featured ⚠️ Limited
Project scheduling ✅ Gantt + calendar ✅ Good
Job costing ✅ Strong ✅ Excellent
Estimating ✅ Full ✅ Full
Time tracking ✅ Included ✅ Included
Subcontractor billing ✅ Good ✅ Strong
QuickBooks integration ✅ Two-way ✅ Two-way
Service/recurring work ⚠️ Limited ✅ Strong
Daily logs ✅ Full ⚠️ Basic
Change order workflow ✅ Excellent ✅ Good
Starting price $499/mo ~$149/mo

Where Buildertrend wins

Residential project management depth

Buildertrend's feature set is built around the residential construction workflow. The client portal is one of the best in the industry — homeowners can view their project schedule, approve change orders, communicate with the team, and pay invoices, all from a branded portal. The daily log and photo documentation tools create a professional record of project progress that clients appreciate and subcontractors are familiar with.

Scheduling and timeline management

Buildertrend's Gantt-style schedule with task dependencies is designed for managing multi-week and multi-month construction projects. Phase-based scheduling, subcontractor scheduling, and client milestone visibility all work well. For GCs managing 10–50 active residential projects simultaneously, this scheduling depth is critical.

Lead management and sales CRM

Buildertrend includes a CRM for managing leads, proposals, and the pre-construction sales process. For residential GCs who generate leads from Houzz, Google, or referrals and need to track them through the sales funnel, this is valuable functionality that Knowify doesn't match.

Ecosystem and integrations

Buildertrend has built a large ecosystem over more than 15 years. Integrations with QuickBooks, Xero, DocuSign, Lowe's, The Home Depot, and dozens of other tools create a connected platform. Its marketplace of third-party integrations is significantly broader than Knowify's.

Where Knowify wins

Job costing precision for specialty trades

Knowify's job costing is its standout strength. Budget vs. actual tracking by cost code, labor cost allocation, material markup management, and profitability reporting are all designed for the way specialty subcontractors actually run jobs. For electrical, HVAC, or plumbing contractors who need to know exactly what they're making on every job, Knowify's cost tracking is detailed and accurate.

Service and recurring work management

Knowify has a service dispatch module designed for recurring work — maintenance contracts, service calls, and scheduled visits. This is a genuine gap in Buildertrend, which is designed for project-based work rather than recurring service. For specialty contractors who do both service and project work, Knowify handles the full mix better.

Price point

Knowify's entry pricing around $149/month is meaningfully lower than Buildertrend's $499/month starting point. For small specialty contractors who don't need Buildertrend's full residential feature set, the cost difference is significant. Knowify delivers most of what a specialty sub needs at roughly a third of the cost.

Subcontractor billing and AIA billing

Knowify handles AIA-style progress billing and retainage management well — important for subcontractors on commercial projects who bill GCs using standard AIA forms. Buildertrend's billing is designed more for homeowner invoicing than commercial payment applications.

Who should choose Buildertrend?

  • Residential general contractors managing new construction or remodeling
  • Companies where client communication and portal access is a priority
  • GCs with 10+ active projects who need robust scheduling and change order management
  • Contractors who want a single platform for CRM through closeout

Buildertrend — The residential GC standard for a reason. Free trial available.

Start Buildertrend Trial →

Who should choose Knowify?

  • Specialty subcontractors (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, painting, etc.) who need strong job costing
  • Contractors who mix project work with recurring service and maintenance
  • Smaller companies (under $5M revenue) where Buildertrend's price is hard to justify
  • Subs who bill GCs using AIA payment applications and need retainage management

The verdict

For residential general contractors, Buildertrend is the stronger platform — the client portal, residential workflow, and scheduling depth justify the price premium. For specialty subcontractors who need job costing precision and service management without residential GC features, Knowify delivers more relevant functionality at a lower price point.

If you're a specialty sub who primarily works as a subcontractor to GCs (rather than managing homeowner relationships directly), Knowify is likely the better fit. If you're a GC managing direct homeowner relationships, Buildertrend wins.

Not sure which fits your workflow? Houzz Pro is also worth considering for design-build and remodeling work — particularly if client experience is a priority.
